basic_details.job_title: BIS Section Officer Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 20 Posts
basic_details.short_information: The Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS), a statutory body under the administrative control of the Government of India, Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution (Department of Consumer Affairs), has released an official advertisement for Section Officer positions.

important_dates.last_date_to_apply: Within 45 days from the date of publication in Employment News/Rozgar Samachar
important_dates.selection process date: Will be intimated separately to shortlisted candidates

age_limit.details: Maximum Age: 56 years as on the closing date of receipt of applications. | Departmental Candidates: BIS's own departmental candidates are not eligible to apply for deputation posts. | Place of Posting: BIS reserves the right to modify the number of posts and place of posting, and may transfer candidates across any BIS office in India as per organizational requirement.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill the minimum qualifications and service conditions specified by BIS: | Option A: Persons holding an analogous post on a regular basis. Option B: Candidates in Level-6 of the Pay Matrix with 3 years of regular service. | Option A: Master of Business Administration (MBA) with Personnel/Finance specialization of two years' duration from a recognized university/AICTE-approved institution. Option B: Post Graduate Degree/Post Graduate Diploma in Personnel Management/Human Resource Management/Finance of two years' duration from a recognized university/AICTE-approved institution.

salary_details.period of deputation: 2 Years (Extendable for another 1 year at BIS's discretion)
salary_details.details: Essential administrative details regarding the vacancy package include:

how_to_apply: Eligible candidates must submit applications through proper channel (Forwarding Authority) via Speed Post only:
Fill the application form in the prescribed proforma given in Annexure-I in capital letters.
Attach attested copies of certificates relating to age, caste/category, qualifications, and experience.
Complete the Declaration (Annexure-II) and ensure the Forwarding Authority countersigns with the certificate.
The Forwarding Authority must enclose: (i) attested copies of last 5 years' APARs, (ii) Vigilance Clearance Certificate, and (iii) Integrity Certificate.

Send the application only by Speed Post, superscribing the envelope: "The application for the post of Section Officer on Deputation", within 45 days from the date of publication of the advertisement in Employment News/Rozgar Samachar, to:
Director (Establishment), Bureau of Indian Standards, Manak Bhavan, 9, Bahadur Shah Zafar Marg, New Delhi – 110002

content_sections.heading: BIS Section Officer Recruitment 2026 Key Dates
content_sections.text: The selection and submission timelines for the post are structured below:

rows.details: Within 45 days from the date of publication in Employment News/Rozgar Samachar
Will be intimated separately to shortlisted candidates

content_sections.heading: BIS Vacancy and Salary Highlights
content_sections.text: Essential administrative details regarding the vacancy package include:

rows.details: 2 Years (Extendable for another 1 year at BIS's discretion)

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria & Job Requirements
content_sections.text: Candidates must fulfill the minimum qualifications and service conditions specified by BIS: Option A: Persons holding an analogous post on a regular basis. Option B: Candidates in Level-6 of the Pay Matrix with 3 years of regular service. Option A: Master of Business Administration (MBA) with Personnel/Finance specialization of two years' duration from a recognized university/AICTE-approved institution. Option B: Post Graduate Degree/Post Graduate Diploma in Personnel Management/Human Resource Management/Finance of two years' duration from a recognized university/AICTE-approved institution.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit & Other Conditions
content_sections.text: Maximum Age: 56 years as on the closing date of receipt of applications. Departmental Candidates: BIS's own departmental candidates are not eligible to apply for deputation posts. Place of Posting: BIS reserves the right to modify the number of posts and place of posting, and may transfer candidates across any BIS office in India as per organizational requirement.

content_sections.heading: BIS Selection Methodology
content_sections.text: The selection process for the Section Officer post is as follows: Initial Screening: Applications will be screened based on eligibility criteria, qualifications, and experience submitted through proper channel. Documents Required with Application: Attested copies of last 5 years' APAR dossiers, Vigilance Clearance Certificate, and Integrity Certificate duly signed by the appropriate authority must be forwarded by the concerned authority along with the application. Final Selection: Based on assessment of applications and documents submitted through proper channel.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for BIS Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Eligible candidates must submit applications through proper channel (Forwarding Authority) via Speed Post only: Director (Establishment), Bureau of Indian Standards, Manak Bhavan, 9, Bahadur Shah Zafar Marg, New Delhi – 110002

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies announced under BIS Section Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) has announced 20 Section Officer vacancies on a deputation basis across various Regional Offices, Branch Offices, and Laboratories in India.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for BIS Section Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Eligible candidates must submit their applications within 45 days from the date of publication of the advertisement in Employment News/Rozgar Samachar.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for BIS Section Officer posts?
faq.answer: Candidates should possess an MBA (Personnel/Finance) or a Post Graduate Degree/Diploma in Personnel Management, Human Resource Management, or Finance from a recognized university or AICTE-approved institution.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for BIS Section Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applicants should not exceed 56 years of age as on the closing date of receipt of applications.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary of a BIS Section Officer?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive pay under Level-7 of the Pay Matrix, with a salary ranging from ₹44,900 to ₹1,42,400.

faq.question: Q6. What is the mode of recruitment for BIS Section Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The recruitment is being conducted on a deputation basis for eligible employees of Central Government, State Government, Union Territory Government, Statutory Bodies, and Autonomous Organizations.

faq.question: Q7. How will candidates be selected for the BIS Section Officer post?
faq.answer: The selection process includes screening of applications, verification of eligibility, qualifications, experience, APAR records, Vigilance Clearance, and Integrity Certificate submitted through the proper channel.

faq.question: Q8. What is the deputation period for BIS Section Officer posts?
faq.answer: The initial deputation period is 2 years, which may be extended by one additional year at the discretion of BIS.
